title: Official vs Fake Apps
fullRewardHint: Answer all {{total}} choices correctly to earn the full reward.
reflectionPrompts: How can you verify the authenticity of financial apps before downloading?
What indicators should you look for to ensure app safety?

stages.prompt: Scenario: How do you identify a safer app?

options.label: Random download link
options.reflection: Random download links are often sources of fake or malicious apps that can compromise your security.

options.label: Official app store + verified developer
options.reflection: Correct! Official app stores have verification processes and verified developers are more trustworthy.

options.label: Most downloaded app
options.reflection: Most downloaded doesn't guarantee safety; fake apps can also gain downloads through manipulation.

options.label: Best rated app
options.reflection: Ratings can be manipulated; official sources and verified developers are more reliable indicators.

stages.prompt: What is the safest way to download a financial app?

options.label: From official app stores like Google Play or Apple App Store
options.reflection: Exactly! Official app stores have security measures to verify app authenticity and safety.

options.label: From third-party websites offering faster downloads
options.reflection: Third-party sites may host modified or malicious versions of the app.

options.label: From social media links shared by friends
options.reflection: Links shared on social media could lead to fake or malicious apps.

options.label: Directly from the company's website
options.reflection: While company websites may be legitimate, official app stores provide additional verification layers.

stages.prompt: What should you look for to verify a financial app's authenticity?

options.label: Verified developer badge and official company name
options.reflection: Perfect! Verified developer badges and official company names help confirm authenticity.

options.label: Number of downloads only
options.reflection: Download numbers don't guarantee authenticity; verification factors are more important.

options.label: High ratings only
options.reflection: Ratings can be manipulated; verified developer status is a more reliable indicator.

options.label: Colorful app icon
options.reflection: Visual appearance doesn't indicate authenticity; verification markers are what matter.

stages.prompt: Why might an official app store be safer than alternative sources?

options.label: They always provide free apps
options.reflection: Official stores contain both free and paid apps; cost doesn't indicate safety.

options.label: They have review processes and security checks for apps
options.reflection: Yes! Official stores review apps and check for known security threats before listing them.

options.label: They have more apps available
options.reflection: Quantity of apps doesn't indicate safety; verification processes do.

options.label: They have better graphics
options.reflection: Visual quality doesn't indicate safety; verification processes and developer checks do.

stages.prompt: What is the best approach to avoid fake financial apps?

options.label: Search for the app name online and download from the first link
options.reflection: The first search result may be a fake site hosting malicious apps.

options.label: Download any app with the company's logo
options.reflection: Logos can be copied; official stores and verified developers are more reliable indicators.

options.label: Only download from official app stores and verify developer identity
options.reflection: Excellent! This combination provides the best protection against fake financial apps.

options.label: Choose the app with the most positive reviews
options.reflection: Reviews can be fabricated; official verification is more reliable than review counts.
